name: Parque Regional Curso Medio del Guadarrama – circular from Villanueva de la Cañada by the Castillo de Aulencia
name_original: Parque Regional Curso Medio del Guadarrama – circular desde Villanueva de la Cañada por el Castillo de Aulencia
region_intro: Touring cycling around Villanueva De La Cañada offers a dedicated infrastructure for exploring the area. The municipality features a 17-kilometer cycle path that winds through open spaces, parks, and gardens, providing accessible routes. The region's appeal for outdoor activities stems from its integration with natural elements, offering a pleasant backdrop for cycling. Varied terrain and scenery are present in the broader area surrounding the town. Best touring cycling routes around Villanueva De La Cañada The most popular touring cycling route is Parque Regional Curso Medio del Guadarrama – circular from Villanueva de la Cañada by the Castillo de Aulencia, a 18.8 miles (30.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 28 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores natural parkland and passes by the Castillo de Aulencia. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Aulencia Castle – Dehesa Camino del Palancar loop from Villanueva de la Cañada, a moderate 26.3 miles (42.3 km) path. This route offers a blend of historical sites and open country scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Villanueva de la Cañada loop from Villanueva de la Cañada, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail leading through local green spaces, often completed in about 45 minutes. Touring cycling around Villanueva De La Cañada is defined by open spaces, parks, and varied terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging distances. The routes in Villanueva De La Cañada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 450 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villanueva De La Cañada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of touring cycling routes are available in Villanueva De La Cañada?
A: Villanueva De La Cañada offers a variety of touring cycling routes, ranging from easy loops to more challenging distances. The municipality features a dedicated 17-kilometer cycle path that winds through open spaces, parks, and gardens, providing accessible and pleasant routes. Overall, there are over 50 touring cycling routes, with 18 easy, 24 moderate, and 8 difficult options.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, there are 18 easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31611171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de la Cañada loop from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.9 miles (12.7 km) long and leads through local green spaces, often completed in about 45 minutes. The 17-kilometer municipal cycle path is also ideal for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are some moderate touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For those looking for a moderate challenge, there are 24 routes available. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962665210/parque-regional-curso-medio-del-guadarrama-circular-from-villanueva-de-la-canada-by-the-castillo-de-aulencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Regional Curso Medio del Guadarrama – circular from Villanueva de la Cañada by the Castillo de Aulencia&lt;/a&gt;, an 18.8-mile (30.3 km) trail exploring natural parkland and passing by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1629985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Aulencia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced touring cyclists, there are 8 difficult routes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14937434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmayor Reservoir – Seat of Philip II loop from Cº Olivar - Urb. Las Velisas&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 44 miles (71.6 km) and features significant elevation changes, offering a demanding ride through varied terrain.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Villanueva De La Cañada?
A: Many touring cycling routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962665210/parque-regional-curso-medio-del-guadarrama-circular-from-villanueva-de-la-canada-by-the-castillo-de-aulencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Regional Curso Medio del Guadarrama – circular from Villanueva de la Cañada by the Castillo de Aulencia&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10377576" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aulencia Castle – Dehesa Camino del Palancar loop from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass by historical sites and natural features. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1629985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aulencia Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a notable landmark. Other points of interest in the broader region include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1575300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Retamar Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/317503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Gasco Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy about cycling in Villanueva De La Cañada?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the integration with natural elements, and the well-maintained cycle paths that make for a pleasant cycling experience.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Villanueva De La Cañada?
A: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling, with milder temperatures. However, the dedicated cycle path and varied terrain mean that cycling is enjoyable for much of the year, avoiding the peak heat of summer or colder winter days.

Q: Are there any routes that combine historical sites with natural scenery?
A: Yes, many routes offer a blend of history and nature. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10377576" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aulencia Castle – Dehesa Camino del Palancar loop from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt; provides views of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1629985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aulencia Castle&lt;/a&gt; alongside open country scenery.

Q: How long are the touring cycling routes typically?
A: The routes vary significantly in length. You can find shorter loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31611171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de la Cañada loop from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt; at 7.9 miles (12.7 km), up to longer, more challenging rides such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14937434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmayor Reservoir – Seat of Philip II loop from Cº Olivar - Urb. Las Velisas&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 44 miles (71.6 km).

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The region offers varied terrain, including dedicated cycle paths, local roads, and paths through open spaces, parks, and gardens. While the 17-kilometer municipal path is generally flat and easy, other routes can include gentle ascents and descents, providing a diverse cycling experience.
